justification: All 7 pass criteria met: (1) Request ID gap identified as #1 must-have, (2) API call duration gap identified as #6 must-have, (3) Metric label valid/invalid gap identified as #3 must-have, (4) Latency histogram absence noted as #4 must-have, (5) PII consideration flagged appropriately in item #11 as 'redact street if PII concern' rather than blindly recommending logging full address, (6) Verdict is NOT READY, (7) Recommendations are highly specific and actionable with numbered items and clear rationale.

observations.text: Request ID gap identified prominently — called out in integration assessment, observability review, and as #1 must-have recommendation
API call duration gap explicitly identified in observability gaps and as #6 must-have
Metric label gap (valid/invalid) explicitly called out with business justification — 'cannot calculate validation success rate'
Latency histogram absence noted as #4 must-have with SLO rationale
PII consideration handled correctly — item #11 says 'redact street if PII concern' rather than blindly recommending logging the full address
Verdict is clearly NOT READY with specific blocking items
Recommendations are exceptionally specific and actionable — numbered, prioritized into must-have/should-have/nice-to-have tiers
Goes beyond minimum rubric with excellent bonus findings: health check liveness/readiness split, circuit breaker absence, rate limiting gaps, graceful degradation as business decision
The 'what on-call sees today vs what they need' comparison is an outstanding way to communicate the logging gaps
Appropriate escalation to Bird/Product Owner for the degradation strategy business decision
